Dear Sir,  


We are pleased to inform you that the Management has approved your firm as Stock Auditor.  

As per Reserve Bank of India guidelines, Stock Audit of all borrowal accounts having working capital limits of Rs 100.00 lakhs and above are to be annually done.  

The inspection of the account assigned to you should be completed and the report thereof should be submitted on or before November 20, 2025. The report should be as per the format prescribed by the Bank. Additional observations based on your analysis should be annexed, wherever necessary.  

The report should be submitted to the following department:  
- 1 Copy to the Audit Department at Corporate Office, Worli.  
- 1 Copy to the Central Documentation and Monitoring Department (CDMD) at Fort/ Surat Regional Office.  
- 1 Copy to the respective branch.  
- 1 Copy to the Risk Department at Corporate Office, Worli  
- 1 Copy to the Credit Department at Corporate Office, Worli.  

The remuneration for the borrower wise assignment will be as under:

Dear Sir,  


Sub.: Stock and Book Debts audit of the Borrowers of the Bank – Rs. 1 Core and above  


We are pleased to assign you audit of hypothecated Stock and Book Debts of borrowers enjoying Working Capital facility (Cash Credit/WCTL/Export Credit) as per list attached, on the terms and conditions mentioned  

As Stock and book debt serves as the primary security for Working Capital facilities, making their quality paramount. Hence, while conducting Stock and Book debt audit, it should be ensured that  
1. Assets are genuine, accurately valued and not overstated.  
2. Receivable are recoverable, not fictitious and inflated.  
3. There is no diversion of fund or double financing (pledging to multiple lenders).  
4. Borrowers maintain robust Internal control to manage inventory and receivable.  


### A. Terms & Conditions  
1. You should physically verify hypothecated stock and condition of storage, viz. Accessibility to godowns, open or covered, owned or rented, in the premises or in godowns/factory of third party etc.  
2. You should reconcile the stock statement submitted by the borrower with the accounting records maintained by borrower, particularly, relating to quantity, rate, value of inventories, age, marketability etc.  
3. You should comment on the valuation, age and holding levels of stock/receivables. While commenting on the valuation of the stock/receivables, you should also comment on matter of material management (such as levels of inventory, credit policy etc.)  
4. You should check the position of old/obsolete/slow moving stocks and their realizable value, if any.  
5. You should comment on the policies relating to procurement of raw materials, management and movement of stock, issue and costing etc.  
6. You should do age wise and value wise analysis of the debtors (receivables), reasons for non-payment of old debts and verification of the debtors (receivables) with records maintained by the borrower.  
7. You should verify and evaluate the sundry creditors, indicating separately those relating to the stock and their relationship with Bank Finance.  
8. You should comment upon the sourcing of the materials i.e. whether any credit is available for the material and which of the items are available against cash payments.
